text: Writing clean code is a skill that helps both you and other developers understand your work. One key principle is making your code readable. Use meaningful naming conventions for variables and functions; for example, instead of naming a variable "x", use "userAge" so it is clear what it stores. Break your code into small, focused functions that each do one job. This makes your program easier to test and modify. Clean code improves maintainability because when you need to add new features or fix bugs, you can quickly see what each part does. Overall, writing clean code saves time and reduces frustration in the long run.
